prevalence.source: Mental health epidemiology syntheses; WHO context
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing with stress and economic factors

incidence.source: Primary care and mental health cohort data

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to chronic symptoms and functional impairment
burden.mortalityRate: Low (indirect via comorbidity and suicide risk)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Persistent excessive worry for >6 months with functional impairment; DSM-5 criteria; exclude medical causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with access to psychotherapy and SSRIs

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care and CAPS
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: CBT when available, SSRIs/SNRIs, lifestyle interventions; avoid long-term benzodiazepines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to psychotherapy and stigma
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care mental health programs and affordable medications
